title: Surgical attention-only realization: real rung above 222, or mirage?
note: All three arms served back-to-back in one pod session (shared sigma_hw). Local AWS A10G, analysis-only; NO HF job, NO submission, NO draw. Surgical lever = LOCAL serve-venv vLLM prototype edit (triton_unified_attention.py honoring SURGICAL_ATTN_USE_3D_OFF=1, one gated line), restored to stock after measurement.

speed.metric: median wall_tps over 3 back-to-back decodes (num_completion_tokens / decode duration_s); round-0 is a cold-start outlier excluded by the median

per_op_tax_decomposition.note: direct measurement of the 481->222 collapse, decomposed by lever (independent check on the ubel #484 per-op attribution ask). Two SEPARABLE costs, not one.
per_op_tax_decomposition.conclusion: the matmul tax is ~56% of the 481->222 collapse and is SEPARABLE and identity-unnecessary (buys only 9 ULP-tie flips). The 2D order-preserving attention tax (~44%, 107 TPS) is the price of byte-exact attention on THIS route and is unavoidable here (the fast 3D split-KV attention is exactly what makes deployed non-equivalent at 0.9966).

mechanism.interpretation: surgical took the 2D order-preserving attention path (no 3D split-KV redirect) while NOT installing the global matmul tax (0 batch_invariant mentions) -- exactly the attention-only lever.

identity.pr_requested_gate: surgical M=8 served vs M=1 AR must be operative-1.0 (>=0.99, all non-tie = bf16-ULP, 0 semantic flips)
identity.why_unmeasurable: The census M=1-AR gate is confounded: (1) the global VLLM_BATCH_INVARIANT=1 config -- batch-invariant BY CONSTRUCTION -- fails the SAME gate identically (full_flag 0.4203 vs surgical 0.4415); a valid gate would pass it. (2) It diffed the cold round-0 served decode against an EAGER (ONEGRAPH-off) M=1 reference; cold-start + eager-vs-cudagraph + M=1-vs-M=8 batching each re-order bf16 reductions, and on 512-token reasoning chains a single ULP flip cascades, flooring the raw rate at the run-to-run noise level.

clean_confound_free_signal.axis_isolated: matmul library only (both arms share 2D order-preserving attention; surgical = fast Marlin, full_flag = batch-invariant tax)

dominant_equivalence_axis.conclusion: the 2D order-preserving ATTENTION (which surgical has) is the byte-equivalence-bearing component; the global flag's matmul tax refines only 9/128 sequences on top of it.

identity.margin_attribution: merged #461: attn-only residual flips are bf16-ULP near-ties (margin 0.125-0.25), not semantic; this census reproduces that identity population in-session.
identity.official_gate_reminder: program.md greedy-identity gate is served-vs-served byte-exact (same serving config), NOT served-vs-M=1-AR-eager; the surgical-vs-full_flag warm comparison is the in-session analogue.

verdict.is_457_a_mirage: partially -- the rung is REAL (+135.7 over 222, 28x the sigma_hw bar) but the ~457 magnitude was a ~22% overshoot; the composed/locus harnesses (#472/#475/#466) over-estimated by ignoring served overhead. Realized surgical = 357.6, not 457.
verdict.reasoning: surgical and the 222 global flag are operatively indistinguishable in identity (warm 0.9763, both perfectly self-deterministic, 9 residual ULP-tie flips per #461) and share the same 2D order-preserving attention. The matmul tax that costs the global flag 135 TPS (357->222) buys only 9/128 sequences of ULP-tie refinement -- no semantic identity gain. So if the 222 global-flag ship is accepted as strict byte-equivalent, surgical attention-only is strict by the identical standard, at 357.6 instead of 222.
verdict.caveat: A definitive operative-1.0 CERTIFICATION would need the organizer's served-vs-served reference at matched config (the actual official gate) or a logit-margin census (#461-style) -- NOT a raw-token M=1-AR decode, which is unmeasurable here for ANY config including the global flag. The byte-equivalence claim rests on the warm same-config cross-arm signal + the attention-axis attribution + #461's margin proof.
